steps.title: Spot the contrast in lifespans
steps.content: The passage directly contrasts two independent clauses: one rockfish species lives about a decade, the other about two centuries. The signal is the contrast between the two lifespans — one very brief, one very long. This tells us which punctuation pattern will properly connect them.

steps.title: Apply the comma + while pattern for contrasting clauses
steps.content:
"While" here means "whereas" — it expresses a direct contrast.

The standard pattern for joining two independent clauses with a contrast word is comma + while: "A, while B" (similar to comma + "but").

The other three options disrupt this pattern with punctuation that either separates the clauses incorrectly or creates ambiguity.

That is the correct answer.
